Petition For Appointment Of Receiver Upon Court Acceptance Of Resignation Of Executor [California Code Of Civil Procedure Section 564(B)(9)]
Off Calendar Nov-10-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: David Little, via ZOOM, for David Bradlow, Proposed Receiver; Paul Perdue, via ZOOM, for Jack Jair, Personal Representative; Carolyn Espey, via ZOOM, for Sylvia Fong Kan and Sharon So-Epprecht, Objectors.

The court queries counsel if they have anything to add regarding the petitions to appoint a Receiver for this and related matters. Ms. Espey reports that she has yet to confirm the bank account numbers shown on the proposed orders submitted to the court. The court advises Ms. Espey that it will not be issuing a blanket order so the account numbers need to be confirmed or this petition will be taken off calendar. The court questions Ms. Espey's request to order Mr. Jair to provide his social security number. Ms. Espey advises that she intended to cover her bases with that request but withdraws the request in open court. Mr. Purdue reports that he could not obtain final confirmation of bank account numbers from his client but advises that there is no objection to the court appointing someone to take over the management of the properties. Mr. Purdue also suggests the court to order the parties to mediation to assist with clarifying the accounts in these matters. The court advises the parties that it will not order the matter to mediation at this time but that it intends to appoint Mr. Bradlow as Receiver and reiterates that the bank account numbers need to be confirmed. Ms. Espey requests to submit a new proposed order to the court or suggests confirming the last four account numbers in open court. The court orders counsel to confer offline and the court takes a brief recess. The court reconvenes and confirms the bank account numbers with Ms. Espey.

After additional discussion, the Petition for Appointment of Receiver, etc., in this matter and related matters is submitted. All remaining petitions on today's calendar for this matter and related matters are continued to Jan-04-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Petition For Appointment Of Receiver Upon Court Acceptance Of Resignation Of Executor [California Code Of Civil Procedure Section 564(B)(9)]
Off Calendar Sep-28-2021 Continued To Nov-09-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Paul Perdue, via ZOOM, for Jack Jair, Personal Representative; Carolyn Espey, via ZOOM, for Hazel So and Sharon So-Epprecht, Objectors; David Little, via ZOOM, for David Bradlow, via ZOOM, Proposed Receiver.

The court begins by advising counsel that it has provided ample time for the parties to resolve this matter and that Jack Jair has demonstrated that he is unable to serve of Executor and that the appointment of a Receiver would assist in moving the case along. Ms. Espey states that in her opinion, petitions to determine heirship in some of these matters may provide the proposed Receiver a better understanding of the matters. Mr. Perdue reports that Jack Jair is his uncle who can be a bit difficult to work with but that the petition to appoint a Receiver has grabbed his attention. Mr. Perdue reports that he would be amenable to mediation. Mr. Purdue advises that there is a need to appraise the properties and that there are pending lawsuits regarding some of the properties. The court then points Mr. Perude to his uncles' failure to account. Mr. Perdue reports that the issue is not that his uncle is refusing to provide accountings but that due to his age, he has issues gathering records. The court then reminds the parties that it has been attempting to have the parties mediate for more than a year. Mr. Perdue advises that he believes that his uncle is willing to pay for private mediation. The court advises the parties that the petition for appointment of receiver is incomplete as it requires a bond proposal and an oath from the receiver. Ms. Espey reports that minimum bond has been requested in the petitions. The parties and the court resume discussions regarding the bond amount being proposed. The court advises that minimum bond will not suffice and needs a more realistic bond proposal from the parties. The court advises that if a receiver is appointed, mediation will be ordered and the power to sell the properties will not be granted. The court encourages the parties to meet and confer to discuss resolving this matter to avoid further litigation. The court confirms that all parties in the trust and estate matters have been notified. The court suggests that in an abundance of caution, the more service the better.

After additional discussion, the matters are continued to Nov-09-2021 at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: C. Andam. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar May-19-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
The following matters were heard: PES-16-299969 Estate of Catherine Jair and
PES-20-303882 Estate of Raymond Jair.

Appearances: Carolyn Espey appeared by zoom for Sharon So Epprecht (Objector in Catherine's estate) and for Sylvia Tan (Objector in Raymond's estate).
Sheila Robello and Vincent Yu appeared by zoom for and with Paul Perdue (Petitioner/Proposed Administrator) by zoom.
Paul Perdue appeared by zoom for Jack Jair (Former Administrator in Catherine's estate).

Ms. Espey referred to the April 16th order requiring Jack Jair to provide bank statements, tax records, etc., stated that some information was provided, but bank statements were not provided, that they found out that Mr. Jair has collected rents and is depositing the rents into property management accounts which include other properties that Jack is managing, and that Mr. Jair has served K-1's for the tax year of 2020, but they do not have a copy of the 2020 tax return. Ms. Espey also stated that overall the court order has not been complied with properly, that what has been provided cannot be connected to anything concrete, that there is about $16,000.00 a month of rental income coming in and that they have no choice other than to have a Receiver appointed. The Court stated that it will not issue an order without filing of a petition for appointment of a Receiver. Ms. Espey requested a continuance and requested that Mr. Perdue's petition for appointment be denied and taken off calendar. The Court noted that this would leave no Administrator and that it makes things worse. Mr. Yu stated that they provided over 200 pages of charts, Mr. Jair's notes, and details on rents, utilities, taxes, and pro rata shares for each Beneficiary. The Court stated that a personal fashion of maintaining records is no longer appropriate once a person becomes a fiduciary, that it is troubled that no work was done until April, questions what happened in March. and stated that it is not impressed with the progress. Mr. Perdue stated that Mr. Jair's CPA couldn't deal with this because of the tax season, that the CPA was retained in the last two years and only has records for 2019 and 2020, and that he requested copies of the tax returns from the IRS around April 20th. Mr. Perdue also stated that he is getting copies of the Bank of America checking account and savings account from Mr. Jair, that they have receipt books for the rents (about three books for each property), that he is prepared to produce everything they have, that he hopes he will get his hands on the bank statements by next week and that the bank accounts are historically where the rents have been deposited. Discussion was held as to why things were to be provided on a rolling basis. The Court stated that Mr. Perdue represents Jack Jair, that there is a significant conflict, that Paul Perdue cannot be appointed as the Administrator of these estates as the conflict runs too deep and that they need someone to step forward and manage these estates. The Court also noted that there are related cases from 1988, stated that the parties need to work together and either file a petition for appointment of a Receiver, an Administrator or something else appointed and that if there is no petition at the next calling, it will consider doing something sua sponte. The order for production remains in place. The Court expects to see that bank records are in Ms. Espey's hands within a week and that things are to be provided on a rolling basis. The Petitions for Letters of Administration in both estates are denied. The substitution of attorney is off calendar. Status on filing of a petition for appointment of a Personal Representative or a Receiver is set for July 14, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.
